title: Align `repo_verify` responses with canonical next-step workflow guidance
summary: Improve the `repo_verify` response contract so an agent following `.rules` can reliably determine what canonical workflow step comes next after verification. This ticket evaluates and, if appropriate, expands `repo_verify` so successful verification clearly exposes resulting transaction status, resulting phase, next action, and whether the workflow should proceed toward commit or return to fix-and-retry behavior.
outputs: A clarified `repo_verify` response contract that exposes canonical workflow next-step guidance where applicable
Consistent verify response fields that help agents interpret verified versus failed states without guessing
Regression tests validating that `repo_verify` response guidance matches canonical transaction state
acceptance_criteria: A successful `repo_verify` response clearly indicates the resulting canonical verification state and the next workflow action expected by the transaction model.
A failed `repo_verify` response clearly indicates that verification did not advance the workflow to commit-ready state and exposes the appropriate next-step guidance for retry or repair.
If `repo_verify` is invoked in an active transaction context, any new workflow-oriented response fields match the persisted canonical `tx_state` after verification completes.
The response remains backward-compatible by preserving existing verification result information such as success indicator, return code, stdout, and stderr while adding workflow guidance fields.
The response does not imply terminal completion or commit readiness when canonical state does not support that conclusion.
Tests fail if `repo_verify` again returns success without enough information for an agent to determine the next canonical workflow step.
tasks: Review the current `repo_verify` return shape and identify which workflow-relevant fields are missing for agent decision-making.
Define a minimal additive response contract that can expose canonical verification outcome, resulting transaction status/phase, next action, and any follow-up requirement.
Implement the new response fields in a way that reflects canonical persisted state rather than local assumptions where practical.
Ensure failure responses continue to include existing diagnostic information while also clarifying that the workflow has not advanced to commit-ready state.
Add or update tests so `repo_verify` response guidance is validated against the canonical transaction state and expected post-verify workflow behavior.
notes: This ticket is lower priority than the commit helper response work because the main ambiguity reported in 0.5.2 is post-commit non-terminal state, but verify response clarity should still be improved for consistency.
The goal is not to redesign verification semantics; it is to make the existing staged workflow more explicit and machine-actionable.
Any added response fields should remain consistent with the current lifecycle distinction between `checking`, `verified`, `committed`, and terminal states.
The agent-facing purpose of this ticket is to reduce guesswork after verification so the next action toward commit or repair can be determined from `.rules` plus tool responses.
